freesasa.macosx-x64: Skipped on Intel mac: macos-14-large runner pool flakes during CPython 3.12 PGO build (intermittent _testcapi failures), and Apple has stopped selling Intel Macs (2023) with macOS 15+ dropping Intel support, so the Intel-mac user base is shrinking. macOS aarch64 (Apple Silicon) still builds freesasa normally.

linux-x64.reason: No cp312 / Linux wheel on PyPI; compile the SWIG-generated freesasa.c on the runner (sdist ships pre-generated C, plain C build via setuptools).

linux-aarch64.reason: No cp312 / Linux ARM64 wheel; compile on the native ARM runner.

macosx-aarch64.reason: No cp312 / macOS arm64 wheel; compile on the runner with clang.

windows-x64.reason: No cp312 / Windows wheel; compile with MSVC. The shared runner workflow activates vcvars via milaboratory/github-ci/actions/setup-msvc-dev-cmd@v4 before this step runs, so the env (INCLUDE / LIB / LIBPATH / PATH plus DISTUTILS_USE_SDK) is in scope when pip wheel invokes setuptools.
